note: GENERATED by scripts/build_sd_history.py — do not edit

artifactNotes.1890-11-04: the paper printed majorities, not raw votes, for all but 3 precincts — the true night count was far higher
artifactNotes.1892-11-08: the morning paper printed no city returns at all — the true night count was far higher
artifactNotes.1900-11-06: city-only total-vote proxy (the countywide table's total row was left blank) — the county night count was higher
artifactNotes.1924-11-04: a remote paper's AP county row at its press time (158 of 274 precincts) — the San Diego Union's own count that night was higher
artifactNotes.1928-11-06: a remote paper's AP county row at its press time (only 31 of 310 precincts had reached the wire) — a severe undercount of the night, not a slow count
artifactNotes.1936-11-03: a pre-dawn EXTRA that went to press with only 167 precincts in — a genuine night count, but an early-press snapshot, so the end-of-night total was higher
